title: Text Object
description: Text annotation on a PCB board, schematic sheet, symbol, or package.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: align
properties.description: Horizontal and vertical alignment of this text.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Rotation angle in degrees, counter-clockwise from the positive X axis (0.0–359.9).

properties.name: font
properties.description: Font style used to render this text (vector, proportional, or fixed).

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: layer
properties.description: Layer number (1-based index into the layer stack).

properties.name: lineDistance
properties.description: Line spacing between lines of text, in internal units.

properties.name: mirror
properties.description: True if this text is mirrored about the Y axis.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: size
properties.description: Text height (character size), in internal units.

properties.name: spin
properties.description: True if the spin flag is set, preventing automatic readability correction when mirrored.

properties.name: x
properties.description: X coordinate of the text anchor point, in internal units.

properties.name: y
properties.description: Y coordinate of the text anchor point, in internal units.
